collection.providerInterface: GitHub REST API through explicit GitHub CLI GET requests
collection.mutationGuard: GET-only; auto-remediation and provider writes are rejected before collection

results.observedValue: main requires pull requests, zero approving reviews under the explicit sole-maintainer exception, seven strict checks, resolved conversations, administrator enforcement, and denies force-push and deletion.
results.diagnostic: The exception avoids a reviewer deadlock without enabling direct push, force push, deletion, or bypass.

results.observedValue: The active tag ruleset retains creation, update, and deletion controls for the prospective release-tag namespace.
results.diagnostic: The ruleset remains prospective and leaves historical tags untouched.

results.observedValue: GitHub reports immutable releases enabled true.
results.diagnostic: The control is prospective and does not rewrite historical releases or tags.

results.observedValue: Each named release environment requires github:furkanmamuk, permits self-review under the explicit sole-maintainer exception, denies administrator bypass, and restricts deployments to protected branches.
results.diagnostic: The repository-wide sole-maintainer exception prevents a deployment deadlock but does not create independent review, release authority, or publication readiness.

results.observedValue: Default workflow token permissions are read and workflows cannot approve pull-request reviews.
results.diagnostic: Repository defaults do not grant implicit write authority.

results.observedValue: GitHub Actions remains enabled, retains the existing all-actions allowance, and now requires full commit SHA pinning.
results.diagnostic: Every declared workflow Action reference is commit-pinned; the provider now rejects mutable action references.

results.observedValue: The approved reversible deploy-key exercise recorded two distinct disposable read-only keys that each authenticated before exact-ID deletion and then produced SSH denial plus a 404 metadata result.
results.diagnostic: The exercise proved a target-scoped provider revocation path without a release, tag, package, deployment, or repository-content effect.
